docs.title: 🚀 Welcome to the Buildy Platform Docs
docs.text: Welcome to the official documentation for the Buildy Platform. Here you’ll find everything you need to get started, run operations, and explore dashboards and APIs.

docs.title: 📖 Quick Start
docs.text:
1. Runbooks → Step-by-step guides for common tasks
  2. Dashboards → Visual insights into your platform metrics
  3. API Reference → Swagger-powered API explorer

👉 Use the navigation bar at the top or the search box (press /) to quickly find what you need.

docs.title: 📂 Documentation Sections
docs.text:
• 🛠️ Runbooks – operational playbooks and troubleshooting steps
  • 📊 Dashboards – analytics and performance dashboards
  • 🔌 API Reference – interactive API explorer

docs.title: 💡 About Buildy
docs.text:
The Buildy Platform is designed to streamline your workflows with:

  • Automation → repeatable, reliable processes
  • Visibility → dashboards and reports that matter
  • Integration → REST API for connecting your tools

docs.title: 🙌 Contributing
docs.text: Found an issue or want to suggest improvements? Contributions are welcome! Check out the GitHub repo.

docs.title: 📬 Support
docs.text:
• Issues → GitHub Issues
  • Docs Feedback → Open a ticket or leave a comment

✨ Tip: Try switching between light 🌞 and dark 🌙 mode with the theme toggle in the header.

docs.title: 🔌 API Reference
docs.text: Welcome to the Buildy Platform API reference. This section provides an interactive way to explore and test API endpoints directly from your browser.

docs.title: 📖 Overview
docs.text:
• Base URL: https://api.buildy.example.com/v1/
  • Authentication: Bearer token (JWT)
  • Format: JSON responses

Use the interactive explorer below to try out requests and see real responses. 👉 Remember to use valid credentials before calling protected endpoints.

docs.title: ⚡ Common Use Cases
docs.text:
• 🔍 Query platform status
  • 👥 Manage users and roles
  • 📊 Fetch metrics for dashboards
  • ⚙️ Automate workflows with scripts

docs.title: 🧭 Interactive API Explorer
docs.text:
The Swagger UI below is generated from our platform.json OpenAPI spec. You can expand each endpoint, view request/response schemas, and even send live requests.

Note

If you’re new to APIs, start with the GET /status endpoint — it’s safe to test and confirms your authentication is working.

docs.title: 🚀 Next Steps
docs.text:
• Review the Runbooks for operational examples.
  • Explore the Dashboards to visualize data from these APIs.

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text: This document provides a high-level view of the V18 Platform architecture.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Frontend: User-facing interface built with modern web technologies.
  • Backend API: Provides business logic and data access.
  • Database: Central storage for structured and unstructured data.
  • Monitoring: Dashboards and alerts ensure system health.
  • CI/CD Pipeline: Automates builds, tests, and deployments.

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
1. User interacts with the frontend.
  2. Frontend makes calls to the backend API.
  3. API queries the database and applies logic.
  4. Results are returned to the user.
  5. Monitoring tools track system behavior at each stage.

docs.title: Diagram (Placeholder)
docs.text: ```text [ User ] → [ Frontend ] → [ API ] → [ Database ] ↓ [ Monitoring ]

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: We welcome contributions to the V18 Platform documentation.

docs.title: How to Contribute
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a new branch
  3. Make your changes
  4. Submit a Pull Request

docs.title: Style Guide
docs.text:
• Use Markdown for content
  • Keep sentences clear and concise
  • Prefer diagrams or lists over long paragraphs

docs.title: Code of Conduct
docs.text: Please be respectful and collaborative. Harassment or abuse will not be tolerated.

docs.title: 📊 Dashboards
docs.text: Dashboards give you real-time insights into the Buildy Platform. They help track performance, spot issues, and measure success.

docs.title: 1. System Health Dashboard
docs.text:
• Purpose: Monitor CPU, memory, disk, and network usage.
  • Users: System admins, on-call engineers.
  • Highlights: Color-coded alerts for high load.

docs.title: 2. Application Performance Dashboard
docs.text:
• Purpose: Track API response times, error rates, and throughput.
  • Users: Developers, SREs.
  • Highlights: Red/green indicators for service health.

docs.title: 3. User Analytics Dashboard
docs.text:
• Purpose: See how users interact with the platform.
  • Users: Product managers, analysts.
  • Highlights: Daily active users (DAU), session length, top features.

docs.title: 📌 Best Practices
docs.text:
• Use filters to drill into specific time ranges.
  • Save frequent views as favorites for faster access.
  • Share dashboards with your team for collaborative troubleshooting.

docs.title: 🛠️ Runbooks
docs.text: Runbooks are your step-by-step guides for common operational tasks. They help ensure consistency, reliability, and speed when managing the Buildy Platform.

docs.title: 1. Restarting a Service
docs.text:
Use when a service becomes unresponsive.

Steps: 1. Log in to the Buildy control panel. 2. Navigate to Services → Active Services. 3. Select the service and click Restart. 4. Verify logs for successful restart.

docs.title: 2. Checking System Health
docs.text:
Use when you suspect performance issues.

Steps: 1. Go to the Dashboards → System Health page. 2. Review CPU, memory, and disk metrics. 3. Check for alerts in the Notifications panel. 4. If issues persist, escalate to the on-call engineer.

docs.title: 3. User Access Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Use when a user reports they cannot log in.

Steps: 1. Confirm username and role in Admin → Users. 2. Check authentication logs. 3. Reset password if necessary. 4. Test login with the user.

docs.title: 📌 Notes
docs.text:
• Keep runbooks short, clear, and actionable.
  • Update them whenever new features are added.

docs.title: Branding
docs.text: Welcome to the Branding section of the documentation. Here you’ll find guidelines, logos, and assets related to the project’s identity.

docs.title: Logo
docs.text: Our official logo is shown below:

docs.title: Usage Guidelines
docs.text:
• Use the logo on light or dark backgrounds without altering proportions.
  • Do not stretch, rotate, or recolor the logo.
  • Always maintain clear space around the logo.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Stylesheets
  • Contributing

docs.title: Brand Colors
docs.text:
Our palette combines modern purple tones with gold highlights. These colors must be used consistently across UI, marketing, and documentation.

Purpose Color Hex Preview Primary Purple #6A0DAD Secondary Gold #FFD700 Background Light Gray #F5F5F5 Text (primary) Dark Gray #333333

docs.title: Accessibility
docs.text:
• Always maintain 4.5:1 contrast ratio for text
  • Use gold as an accent, not a background for text

docs.title: 🎨 Branding
docs.text:
Buildy’s docs are customized with:

  • Logo: { width=120px }

  • Favicon: { width=20px }

  • Colors: Purple + Gold theme for a clean, professional look.

docs.title: Typography
docs.text: Typography shapes our communication style. We use clean, modern, and accessible fonts.

docs.title: Primary Font
docs.text:
• Roboto (Sans-serif)
  • For UI, body text, and documentation

docs.title: Secondary Font
docs.text:
• Montserrat (Sans-serif)
  • For headings, highlights, and call-outs

docs.title: Hierarchy
docs.text:
• H1: Page titles (Montserrat, 32px, bold)
  • H2: Section headers (Montserrat, 24px, medium)
  • Body text: Roboto, 16px, regular
  • Captions/Notes: Roboto, 14px, light

docs.title: Usage Guidelines
docs.text:
• Limit to two fonts only (no substitutions)
  • Maintain consistent line spacing (1.5x body size)
  • Avoid excessive bold or italics
